questions.text: Which wallet injects window.ethereum for MST mainnet (Chain ID 4646)?
questions.explanation: BridgeKey is MST’s native wallet and the recommended extension for dApps on MST mainnet.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ B - BridgeKey

BridgeKey is MST’s native wallet and the recommended extension for dApps on MST mainnet.

questions.text: In a Vite React project, how do you access an environment variable named CONTRACT_ADDRESS?
questions.explanation: Vite requires the VITE_ prefix and uses import.meta.env.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ A

Vite requires the VITE_ prefix and uses import.meta.env.

questions.text: Which of the following is the correct URL to view a transaction on MST mainnet explorer?
questions.explanation: MST Explorer uses the /tx/ path. Option C is fictional.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ A

MST Explorer uses the /tx/ path. Option C is fictional.

questions.text: A React dApp connected to MST mainnet must use a different library than ethers.js because MST is not EVM-compatible.
The README of a production dApp should include the live demo URL and the contract addresses on mainnet.
"Deploying a React frontend to IPFS guarantees that the dApp is fully decentralised with no central points of failure."
questions.explanation: 📊 2 marks: 1 for FALSE, 1 for mentioning RPC dependency.
questions.modelAnswer: 📊 2 marks: 1 for FALSE, 1 for mentioning RPC dependency.

questions.text: "Hardcoding the contract address directly in the React component is an acceptable practice for production dApps."
questions.explanation: 📊 2 marks: FALSE + justification that covers reusability and environment config.
questions.modelAnswer: 📊 2 marks: FALSE + justification that covers reusability and environment config.

questions.text: List four items that must be present in a professional capstone README for an MST BuildGrant application.
questions.explanation: Live demo URL, mainnet contract addresses (verified), local setup instructions, security audit summary or report link. (any four valid)
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Model Answer

Live demo URL, mainnet contract addresses (verified), local setup instructions, security audit summary or report link. (any four valid)

2 marks: 0.5 per correct item.

questions.text: Why is it important to show a transaction link to MST Explorer after a user performs an on-chain action?
questions.explanation: It builds trust by allowing the user to independently verify the transaction on-chain. It also helps debugging and provides a permanent record. Without it, the user has to blindly trust the frontend.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Model Answer

It builds trust by allowing the user to independently verify the transaction on-chain. It also helps debugging and provides a permanent record. Without it, the user has to blindly trust the frontend.

2 marks: trust/transparency + verification.

questions.text:
Submit a production-ready portfolio page that includes:
• A live URL (Vercel/Netlify/IPFS) of your dApp working on MST mainnet (Chain ID 4646).
• A GitHub repository with a complete README (following the checklist above).
• A 5-minute demo video (YouTube link or Loom).
• Screenshot of the deployed contract verified on MST Explorer.

Evaluation criteria:
questions.explanation:
3 marks: Live dApp works (wallet connects, at least one read & write function works).
          2 marks: README complete with contract addresses, setup, and architecture diagram.
          1 mark: Demo video shows core user flow and mentions MST features.
          1 mark: Verified contract screenshot included in the repo.
questions.modelAnswer:
📊 Marks Breakdown

3 marks: Live dApp works (wallet connects, at least one read & write function works).
2 marks: README complete with contract addresses, setup, and architecture diagram.
1 mark: Demo video shows core user flow and mentions MST features.
1 mark: Verified contract screenshot included in the repo.

Total: 7 marks. Partial credit for incomplete submission.
